nodes.title: Track whether the function already ran
nodes.why: This flag is the gate that makes the wrapper run the original function only once.
nodes.alternative: Delete or replace the inner function after the first call.
nodes.tradeoff: Very easy to follow, with one small piece of closure state.

nodes.title: Cache the first return value
nodes.why: Later calls should return the same answer without calling `fn` again.
nodes.alternative: Return `undefined` on later calls instead of caching.
nodes.tradeoff: More useful API, but it keeps the first result in memory.

nodes.title: Call the original function with current context and args
nodes.why: Using `apply` keeps `this` and the full argument list intact.
nodes.tradeoff: More correct for method-style usage, with a slightly less minimal call site.
